name: managing-project-rules
description: Creates and updates modular project rules for Claude Code in .claude/rules/ directory. Use when creating, updating, or modifying rule files, organizing project guidelines, setting up code standards, or when user mentions "create rules", "update rules", "add rules", or "rule configuration".
capabilities: Creates new rule files in .claude/rules/ directory following best practices
Updates existing rule files while preserving structure and content
Organizes rules with path-specific frontmatter for conditional application
Uses subdirectories to group related rules by topic (frontend, backend, general)
Validates YAML frontmatter syntax for path-specific rules
Reports file locations, scope, and changes made
author: Emz1998
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The scanner detected markdown code formatting backticks as Ruby/shell execution and documentation references to symlinks/crypto as security issues. This is a legitimate documentation skill for Claude Code's modular rules feature. No actual code execution, file system operations, or cryptographic operations occur.
valueStatement: Managing multiple project rules across different files is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill organizes Claude Code instructions into modular files using the .claude/rules/ directory, making rules focused, maintainable, and team-friendly.
limitations: Does not execute code or run external commands
Does not modify files outside the .claude/rules/ directory
Does not create symlinks or modify filesystem structure
Does not validate external dependencies or imports

useCases.targetUser: Development teams establishing project-wide coding conventions
useCases.title: Set up coding standards
useCases.description: Create focused rule files for code style, testing requirements, and security guidelines. Each rule file covers one topic for easy maintenance.

useCases.targetUser: Large projects with multiple codebases or services
useCases.title: Organize multi-module project rules
useCases.description: Use subdirectories in .claude/rules/ to separate frontend, backend, and general rules. Apply path-specific rules only when needed.

useCases.targetUser: Developers maintaining and evolving project guidelines
useCases.title: Update existing project rules
useCases.description: Extend existing rule files by adding new sections, removing duplicates, and preserving valuable content. Consult user before major changes.

promptTemplates.title: Create a new rule file
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a rule file for [TOPIC] in .claude/rules/. The rule should cover [KEY_POINTS].
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to add a new focused rule for a specific aspect of the project

promptTemplates.title: Add path-specific rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a path-specific rule for [PATTERN] that covers [REQUIREMENTS]. Use YAML frontmatter to scope the rule.
promptTemplates.scenario: When rules should only apply to certain file types or directories

promptTemplates.title: Organize rules with subdirectories
promptTemplates.prompt: Organize existing rules into a subdirectory structure. Create [SUBDIRECTORY]/[TOPIC].md for [RULES].
promptTemplates.scenario: When rules need better organization for a growing project

promptTemplates.title: Update and extend existing rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Update the existing rule file at .claude/rules/[FILENAME]. Add information about [NEW_CONTENT]. Preserve existing content.
promptTemplates.scenario: When expanding or improving an existing rule file

bestPractices: Keep each rule file focused on one topic like testing, security, or API design
Use descriptive filenames in kebab-case that indicate the rule's purpose
Apply path-specific frontmatter only when rules truly apply to specific file types
Use subdirectories to group related rules (frontend/, backend/, general/)
antiPatterns: Creating one large file that covers multiple unrelated topics
Using path-specific rules for rules that apply to the entire project
Making frequent structural changes without consulting the team
Overlapping rules that contradict or duplicate each other

faq.question: What is the difference between CLAUDE.md and .claude/rules/?
faq.answer: CLAUDE.md is a single file for all project instructions. The .claude/rules/ directory allows splitting instructions into multiple focused files. Both are loaded with equal priority.

faq.question: How do path-specific rules work?
faq.answer: Add YAML frontmatter with a paths field containing glob patterns. The rule only applies when Claude works on files matching those patterns.

faq.question: Can I share rules across multiple projects?
faq.answer: Yes. You can symlink common rule files or directories into .claude/rules/. Symlinks are resolved and loaded normally.

faq.question: What happens with conflicting rules?
faq.answer: Rules are loaded alphabetically. When conflicts occur, the most specific rule (with path frontmatter) takes precedence for matching files.

faq.question: How do user-level rules work?
faq.answer: Create rules in ~/.claude/rules/ in your home directory. User rules load before project rules, giving project rules higher priority.

faq.question: How many rule files should I create?
faq.answer: Create one file per topic. Start with general.md and add specialized files (testing.md, security.md, api.md) as your project grows.
